evals.prompt: Write a SwiftUI view for a user profile screen. It should show the user's avatar, name, email, and bio. Include a loading state while the profile fetches and an error state if it fails. The data comes from a ProfileViewModel.
evals.expected_output: A SwiftUI ProfileView with @StateObject or @ObservedObject for the ProfileViewModel, AsyncImage or placeholder for avatar, loading/error/content states, and proper SwiftUI modifiers.
expectations: Code handles loading state (ProgressView or similar)
Code handles error state with an error message
Code shows avatar, name, email, and bio when loaded
Code uses @ObservedObject or @StateObject for the view model
Code is complete and compilable Swift

evals.prompt: Create a Jetpack Compose screen for a settings page with a dark mode toggle, notification preferences toggle, and a sign out button. Use a ViewModel to hold the state. The app uses Hilt for dependency injection.
evals.expected_output: A Jetpack Compose SettingsScreen composable with ViewModel, two Switch/Toggle preferences for dark mode and notifications, a sign-out button, and Hilt ViewModel injection via hiltViewModel().
expectations: Code includes a SettingsViewModel with Hilt @HiltViewModel annotation
Dark mode and notification toggles are implemented (Switch composable)
State is managed through the ViewModel (not local remember state for persistent settings)

evals.prompt: Build a Flutter screen for a product list with search. Items come from an API. Use Riverpod for state management. Each product shows an image, name, and price. Tapping a product navigates to its detail screen.
evals.expected_output: Flutter ProductListScreen using ConsumerWidget, a Riverpod provider for fetching products, ListView with product cards showing image/name/price, search TextField, and navigation to detail screen.
expectations: Code uses Flutter Widget and ConsumerWidget
Code uses Riverpod (StateNotifierProvider or FutureProvider) for product data
ListView or GridView shows product cards with image, name, and price
Navigation to detail screen is implemented (Navigator.push or go_router)
Loading and error states are handled
